feature.name: Autonomy Escalation Engine V2
feature.summary: Unified autonomy engine with contextual intelligence, shadow execution, impact-weighted demotion, explainability layer, trust capital metric, and role-based presets. 60's most defensible moat — every other V2 system depends on this.

stories.title: Unified trust signal taxonomy and config table
acceptance: Create `autonomy_signal_taxonomy` table: signal_type (PK), category (feedback|outcome|system), base_weight NUMERIC, impact_multiplier_enabled BOOLEAN, description TEXT, is_positive BOOLEAN

Seed all signal types from both systems: approved (+1.0), approved_edited (+0.3), rejected (-1.0), expired (-0.2), undone (-2.0), auto_executed (+0.1), auto_undone (-3.0), rubber_stamp (0.0), context_escalation (-0.5, new), shadow_match (+0.05, new)
Create `autonomy_signal_taxonomy_overrides` table for org-level weight customization: org_id, signal_type, custom_weight
RPC `get_signal_taxonomy(org_id)` returns merged taxonomy (base + org overrides)
Migration is additive — existing autopilot_signals table unchanged

stories.title: Unified autonomy resolver entry point
acceptance: New `resolveAutonomy()` function that consults both System A (org policy) and System B (user confidence) in a single call
Resolution precedence: (1) org policy ceiling, (2) user autopilot tier, (3) org preset default — returns the MORE restrictive of org ceiling vs user tier
Returns AutonomyDecision: { tier, source, orgPolicy, userTier, confidenceScore, factors[], explanation }
Wire into fleetRouter.ts replacing direct autonomyResolver.ts call
Wire trust_history factor into confidenceScorer.ts (completes TODO CC11) — query action_trust_scores for user's approval/rejection history
5-minute cache preserved, invalidation on policy or confidence changes

stories.title: Wire autonomy resolver into chat execution path
acceptance: AutonomousExecutor calls unified resolver before executing each skill tool_use
If tier is 'approve': return proposed action as structured response instead of executing, with approve/edit/reject buttons
If tier is 'suggest': return suggestion text only, no action buttons
If tier is 'disabled': skip the tool call and explain why
If tier is 'auto': execute as before (no change to current behavior)
Record signal (approved/rejected/edited) when user responds to approval prompt

stories.title: Context risk scoring function
acceptance: New `calculateContextRisk()` function returns 0.0-1.0 risk score from deal/contact context
5 context signals with configurable weights: deal_value (0.30), contact_seniority (0.25), deal_stage (0.20), relationship_warmth (0.15), action_reversibility (0.10)
Deal value thresholds: <$25K = low (0.0), $25K-$100K = medium (0.5), >$100K = high (1.0) — configurable per org
Contact seniority from job_title parsing: IC (0.0), Manager (0.3), Director (0.5), VP (0.7), C-suite (1.0)
Returns ContextRisk: { score, factors: { signal, value, weight, contribution }[], escalation_recommendation }

stories.title: Context-aware tier adjustment in unified resolver
acceptance: Unified resolver accepts optional context: { dealId, contactId, dealValue, contactTitle }
When context risk > 0.7 AND current tier is 'auto': downgrade to 'approve' for this action only (transient, not persisted)
When context risk > 0.9: downgrade to 'suggest' regardless of tier
Context escalation recorded as 'context_escalation' signal in autopilot_signals for learning
AutonomyDecision gains `contextRisk` and `contextAdjusted` fields

stories.title: Context-aware execution tier in Command Centre
acceptance: trustScorer.classifyExecutionTier() accepts optional context_risk parameter
When context_risk > 0.7: raise auto_threshold by 0.10 (harder to auto-execute high-value deals)
actionDrafter passes deal/contact context to trust scorer when drafting actions
command_centre_items gains context_risk_score column for audit
Existing items without context default to context_risk = 0.0 (no behavior change)

stories.title: Confidence explanation model (Show Your Work)
acceptance: New `generateExplanation()` function builds human-readable reasons for every tier decision
Explanation template per scenario: 'Auto-sending because: 94% approval rate on follow-ups, $12K deal (low risk), your agent has handled 47 similar actions'
Escalation template: 'Asking for approval because: deal value $250K (high risk), C-suite contact, your edit rate on similar emails is 35%'
Demotion template: 'Reduced to suggest because: 2 undos in last 7 days on email sends, 30-day cooldown active'
Returns ExplanationPayload: { summary (1 line), factors[] (bullet points), recommendation, next_milestone (what it takes to promote) }

stories.title: Show Your Work UI component
acceptance: AutonomyExplanation component: collapsible inline card showing confidence breakdown
Renders explanation factors as a mini progress bar per factor (deal risk, approval history, confidence score)
Shown in: (1) Command Centre items, (2) Copilot approval prompts, (3) Slack approval messages
Next milestone callout: 'Approve 3 more follow-ups to unlock auto-send for this action type'
Compact mode for Slack (plain text with emoji indicators)

stories.title: Autonomy audit trail viewer
acceptance: AutonomyTimeline component: vertical timeline of all tier changes with explanations
Each event shows: timestamp, action type, old tier → new tier, trigger reason, explanation factors
Filter by action type, date range, event type (promotion/demotion/manual/context_escalation)
Embedded in AutonomySettingsPage as a new tab: 'Audit Trail'
Sources data from both autonomy_audit_log (System A) and autopilot_events (System B)

stories.title: Role-based autonomy preset definitions and migration
acceptance: New preset type 'role' in agent_config_defaults alongside existing 'conservative/balanced/autonomous'

4 role presets: sdr (aggressive — auto for task/enrich/slack, approve for email/crm), ae (balanced — approve for email/crm, suggest for proposals), vp_sales (oversight — suggest for most, approve for tasks/slack), cs (renewal-focused — auto for task/enrich/email-followup, approve for proposals)
Each preset includes description, recommended_for text, and icon_name
Existing 3 presets remain as 'style' type — role presets are additive
RPC get_autonomy_presets(type) returns filtered list

stories.title: Role-based preset selector UI
acceptance: Replace generic preset selector with two-step: (1) Choose your role (SDR/AE/VP Sales/CS), (2) Fine-tune style (Conservative/Balanced/Autonomous)
Role selection pre-fills action grid with role-appropriate defaults; style adjusts intensity
Show role description and 'recommended for' text to help users self-identify
First-time setup: role selector shown prominently; returning users see current role with 'Change' button
Non-admin users see their personal role selection (if org allows user overrides)

stories.title: Shadow execution recorder
acceptance: Create `autonomy_shadow_executions` table: id, org_id, user_id, action_type, actual_tier, shadow_tier (one level higher), action_snapshot JSONB, user_decision (approved/approved_edited/rejected), edit_distance, would_have_matched BOOLEAN, created_at
When an action executes at 'approve' tier: also record what would happen at 'auto' tier — save the action snapshot
When user approves without edit: mark would_have_matched = true (shadow execution would have been correct)
When user edits or rejects: mark would_have_matched = false
Runs passively — no additional AI calls, just records the comparison

stories.title: Shadow execution analytics and promotion evidence UI
acceptance: ShadowExecutionInsight component: 'If you'd been on auto for follow-ups last month, 94% would have been sent unchanged'
Shown in: (1) promotion suggestion Slack DMs as evidence, (2) AutonomySettingsPage as a promotion nudge banner, (3) AutopilotDashboard per-action breakdown
RPC `get_shadow_execution_stats(user_id, action_type, days)` returns { total, would_have_matched, match_rate, action_type }
Promotion suggestion only surfaces when shadow match rate > 90% AND total > 10
Clicking 'See evidence' expands to show the last 5 shadow comparisons

stories.title: Impact-weighted demotion scoring
acceptance: Extend demotionEngine severity rules to factor deal value and action reversibility
Impact multiplier: base_demotion_weight * (1 + deal_value_factor + seniority_factor + reversibility_factor)
Reversibility factor: task_create = 0.0 (easily deleted), email_send = 0.8 (can't unsend), crm_stage_change = 0.3 (revertible)
High-impact demotion (multiplier > 2.0) triggers EMERGENCY severity regardless of frequency
Demotion audit log records impact_multiplier and component factors

stories.title: Impact weights config UI and demotion explanation
acceptance: New 'Safety Rules' section in AutonomySettingsPage (admin only)
Configurable deal value thresholds (low/medium/high boundaries) per org
Reversibility factor display: table showing action types and their reversibility rating (read-only, platform-defined)
When demotion occurs, Slack DM includes impact breakdown: 'Demoted email auto-send because: undo on $180K deal (high impact, multiplier 2.8)'
Uses existing Slack DM infrastructure from demotionEngine

stories.title: Trust Capital score calculation
acceptance: New `calculateTrustCapital()` function returns a composite score representing the user's training investment

Components: total_signals (all time), action_types_trained (distinct), avg_confidence_score, days_active, auto_tier_count (action types at auto), personalization_depth (learned preferences from user_ai_preferences)
Trust Capital number: weighted sum normalized to 0-1000 scale for easy display
Switching cost estimate: 'New platforms start at zero. Your agent has X weeks of training.'
Store as materialized view or RPC for fast reads — no separate table needed

stories.title: Trust Capital dashboard component
acceptance: TrustCapital card component: shows score (0-1000), circular progress ring, and breakdown tooltip
Breakdown: 'X signals given across Y action types over Z days. W action types at full auto.'
Switching cost callout: 'Your agent has learned from 847 decisions. New platforms start at zero.'
Embedded in AutopilotDashboard (personal view) and TeamAutopilotView (team averages)
Milestone celebrations: toast when crossing 100, 500, 1000 signals

stories.title: Integration tests for unified resolver and context scoring
acceptance: Vitest suite for unified resolver: org ceiling wins over user tier, context risk downgrades auto to approve, cache invalidation on policy change
Vitest suite for context risk scorer: deal value thresholds, contact seniority parsing, composite risk calculation
Vitest suite for impact-weighted demotion: high-value deal undo triggers emergency, low-value task undo stays at warn
Vitest suite for shadow recorder: records comparison on approve-tier actions, correctly marks would_have_matched
All existing autonomyResolver tests continue passing (backwards compatibility)

parallelGroups.reason: All consume context risk scorer, no file overlap

mvp.delivers: Unified resolver with contextual autonomy and explainability — the core engine
